name: BURKITT LYMPHOMA
definition: highly aggressive B-cell lymphoma usually found in children or immunocompromised adults NONENDEMIC FORM OF BURKITT LYMPHOMA typically manifests with bulky disease because of its rapid doubling time

demographics.Prevalence: 1-2% of all NHLs; 1-5% of primary gastrointestinal NHLs in adults
demographics.Histo: uniform deeply basophilic medium-sized cells containing round nuclei with distinct
demographics.Genetics: translocation of c-Myc oncogene with one of immuno- globulin genes, most
demographics.Associated with: Epstein-Barr virus infection in 95% (implicated as B-cell mitogen in

discriminating_features: "sandwich" sign = enhancing vessels surrounded by mildly enhancing confluent
isointense to muscle on T1WI + T2WI:

US: large hypoechoic masses ± engulfing of bowel / mesenteric vessels
CT: "sandwich" sign = enhancing vessels surrounded by mildly enhancing confluent
MR: isointense to muscle on T1WI + T2WI:
bright round-to-ovoid lesions > 10 mm in size with restricted diffusion ← lymph
Mandible > maxilla / facial bones: grossly destructive lesion, spicules of bone growing at right angles
Other skeleton (multifocal in 10%): reminiscent of Ewing tumor / reticulum cell sarcoma
lamellated periosteal reaction around major long bones
Gastrointestinal tract (22-69%): well-defined sharply marginated homogeneous large abdominal and pelvic masses
encasement of bowel and mesenteric vessels
peritoneal thickening / nodularity (42%) along liver capsule and peritoneal reflections
usually intraabdominal extranodal involvement with sparing of spleen
displacement of bowel loops by a large mass
abnormally separated bowel loops ← extensive bowel wall thickening
Abdominal organs: multiple hypoechoic / hypoattenuating lesions of liver + spleen
Genitourinary tract: multiple hypoechoic / hypoattenuating renal masses / diffuse renal enlargement (5%)
retroperitoneal Burkitt may extend into spinal canal
CNS: epidural spinal mass ± spinal cord compression

clinical.Prognosis: 90-98% 5-year survival rate in children with localized disease: 75-89% 2-year
